questions.prompt_html:
An object moves along the x-axis so that at time t seconds (where t ≥ 0) it is x =  m to the right of the origin. By calculating the average velocity of the object over time intervals [4, 4 + h] for h = 0.1, h = 0.01, 
h = 0.001, and h = 0.0001, determine the velocity of the particle at time t = 4s.
questions.prompt_text: An object moves along the x-axis so that at time t seconds (where t ≥ 0) it is x = square root of (t) m to the right of the origin. By calculating the average velocity of the object over time intervals [4, 4 + h] for h = 0.1, h = 0.01, h = 0.001, and h = 0.0001, determine the velocity of the particle at time t = 4s.

questions.prompt_html: An object moves along the x-axis so that its velocity at time t seconds is v(t) =  m/s. What is the average acceleration (i.e., average rate of change of the velocity) of the object over the time interval [2, 2 + h]? What is the acceleration of the object at time t = 2s?
questions.prompt_text: An object moves along the x-axis so that its velocity at time t seconds is v(t) = (2/3t) m/s. What is the average acceleration (i.e., average rate of change of the velocity) of the object over the time interval [2, 2 + h]? What is the acceleration of the object at time t = 2s?

choices.label: D
choices.html: -  m/s2, -  m/s2

choices.html: m/s2,  m/s2

questions.prompt_html: In the following figure the curve is the graph of a quantity y that is a function of time t. Also shown is its tangent line at t = 100. What is the average rate of change of y from t = 20 to t = 160? What is the approximate rate of change of y with respect to t at t = 100?
questions.prompt_text: In the following figure the curve is the graph of a quantity y that is a function of time t. Also shown is its tangent line at t = 100. What is the average rate of change of y from t = 20 to t = 160? What is the approximate rate of change of y with respect to t at t = 100? Short Description: A graph of a function shows a concave up curve with a tangent line to the curve. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 40 to 200 in increments of 20, and the y axis ranges from negative 30 to 90 in increments of 10. A concave up smooth curve passes through the points (negative 40, 60), (0, 28), (20, 18), (100, 18), and (160, 60). A tangent line to curve passes through the point (100, 18) on the curve and intersects the x axis approximately at (55, 0).

choices.label: A
choices.html: ,

choices.label: B
choices.html: ,

choices.label: C
choices.html: ,

choices.label: D
choices.html: ,

A concave up smooth curve passes through the points (negative 40, 60), (0, 28), (20, 18), (100, 18), and (160, 60). A tangent line to curve passes through the point (100, 18) on the curve and intersects the x axis approximately at (55, 0).
